title: Unicode Normalization, Case Folding, and Original Evidence · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved Bookbot run contains one plausible path, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, reporting, CLI, testing, performance, packaging, security, or release defect; identify the first failed boundary before changing code. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in versioned reversible Unicode policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Choose NFC, NFD, NFKC, NFKD, or no normalization from the comparison goal and version the decision.
Use casefold for declared caseless matching and test expansions and language-sensitive limitations rather than assuming lower is equivalent.
Retain original spans beside normalized comparison keys so reports remain inspectable and reversible.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Unicode Version Evidence
steps.instruction:
Order the Unicode Version Evidence Bookbot and Python corpus-to-release evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Unicode Version Evidence.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Unicode Version Evidence assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Bookbot and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Unicode Version Evidence, keep Unicode Version Evidence connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Bookbot team handles case 1723 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to version normalization and case policy while preserving inspectable original text; keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record Unicode database and policy versions and rerun a changed fixture when either changes.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Unicode Version Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-unicode-version-evidence-bin5s3" that uses case 8831 to version normalization and case policy while preserving inspectable original text. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Unicode Version Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Unicode Version Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Unicode Version Evidence, keep Unicode Version Evidence connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-unicode-version-evidence-bin5s3
# Competency: bookbot-unicode-version-evidence.
# Case 8831: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def evidence_bookbot_unicode_version_evidence_bin5s3(original_units: int = 9, normalized_units: int = 8, form: str = "NFC", original_kept: bool = True, unicode_version_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_bin5s3 = "bin5s3-3s5nib-8831"
    assert evidence_variant_bin5s3
    evidence_axes_bin5s3 = (
        "corpusbin5s3",
        "policy3s5nib",
        "revision8831",
        "commandbin5s38831",
        "report3s5nib8831",
        "test8831bin5s3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_bin5s3)) == 6
    if original_units < 0 or normalized_units < 0:
        return False, normalized_units, "unicode-count"
    if form not in {"NFC", "NFD", "NFKC", "NFKD", "NONE"}:
        return False, normalized_units, "normalization-form"
    if not original_kept:
        return False, normalized_units, "lost-evidence"
    if not unicode_version_kept:
        return False, normalized_units, "unversioned-policy"
    assert original_units >= 0
    return True, normalized_units, "reversible-unicode"
